Off Calendar Apr-14-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF EYAL NAVON's Motion To Compel Innovius Capital Canopus I, L.P.'s Person Most Qualified Deposition; Request For Monetary Sanctions Pursuant To CCP Sections 2023.010 Et Seq. And 2025.450(G)(1).

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion granted, in part, no opposition filed. Innovius Capital Canopus I, L.P.'s person most knowledge shall appear and testify at deposition within 7 days' notice of order. However, the request for sanctions is denied without prejudice. The request for sanctions must be accompanied by a declaration setting forth facts supporting the amount of any monetary sanctions sought. C.C.P. Sec. 2023.040. Here, Mr. Kritzer's declaration states "To date, Mr. Navon has accrued roughly $10,000 in fees on the preparation and filing of the instant motion," which is insufficient to support the monetary relief sought.

Counsel for prevailing party to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at aaron@minnisandsmallets.com (cc'ing opposing counsel).

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint **Redacted**
Off Calendar Mar-24-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS EYAL NAVON's Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Eyal Novan's motion for leave to file a third amended complaint is granted.

The court may permit a party to amend its pleadings in the furtherance of justice. CCP 473(a)(1); CCP 576. Courts have broad discretion in this regard and the discretion is usually exercised liberally to permit amendment. Howard v. County of San Diego (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1422, 1428. The relief may be declined where there is prejudice or long unexplained delay. E.g., Tung v. Chicago Title Company (2021) 63 Cal.App.5th 734, 747 (prejudice). The opposition does not even attempt such a showing, and it is frivolous. The motion is granted, and a new complaint must be filed and served not later than April 3, 2023.

Order signed.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/CK)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-08-2022 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Hippo Holdings, Inc.'s demurrers to the first amended complaint are sustained with leave to amend. Hippo is nowhere mentioned in the FAC's claim for declaratory relief. Likewise, the FAC pleads no fraud allegations against Hippo.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-08-2022 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Innovius Capital Canopus I, L.P.'s demurrers to the first amended complaint are overruled. The FAC's "first claim for relief" seeks, inter alia, declarations that a loan agreement violated usury law. This raises questions of fact not cognizable by demurrer. (Burr v. Capital Reserve Corp. (1969) 71 Cal.2d 983, 989.) The FAC's "eighth claim for relief" seeks another remedy (damages) for violations of the same primary right, so the two claims for relief form one "cause of action." (See Skrbina v. Fleming Companies (1996) 45 Cal.App.4th 1353, 1364.) A demurrer does not lie to only part of a cause of action. (Elder v. Pacific Bell Telephone Co. (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 841, 856 n.14.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
